req.title: External ACH transfer
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-004 defines a tiered ACH transfer system with a $10,000 per-transfer limit, requiring step-up verification for amounts exceeding this threshold. The test suite covers the happy path (valid transfer within limit), boundary conditions (at and above the $10,000 threshold), and error scenarios (insufficient funds, missing verification). Each test is designed using ISO 29119 techniques to ensure deterministic, traceable validation of the requirement's decision logic and error handling.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_within_limit_success
preconditions: ACH account link exists and is verified
No pending disputes on ACH link
expected: ACH batch record created in ledger
trace: R-FIN-004: Positive case – transfer within $10,000 limit
ISO 29119: Equivalence Partition – valid amount in range [1, 10000]
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_ach_transfer_within_limit_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 5000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_123',
        'customer_id': 'cust_verified_001',
        'description': 'Test ACH transfer'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'transfer_id' in data
    assert data['status'] == 'queued'
    assert data['amount'] == 5000
    assert 'batch_id' in data

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_without_stepup
preconditions: ACH account link exists and is verified
Customer has NOT completed step-up verification
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=$12000, beneficiary_id=<valid_ach_id>, customer_id=<verified_customer>, stepup_token=<absent>
expected: Error message includes '$10,000 limit' and 'step-up verification required'
trace: R-FIN-004: Negative case – amount exceeds $10,000 without step-up verification
ISO 29119: Decision Table – (amount > 10000 AND stepup_verified=false) → 422 reject
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_without_stepup():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 12000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_123',
        'customer_id': 'cust_verified_001',
        'description': 'Over-limit transfer without stepup'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_LIMIT_NO_STEPUP'
    assert 'step-up' in data['message'].lower()
    assert 'transfer_id' not in data

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_boundary_10000_success
preconditions: ACH account link exists and is verified
No pending disputes on ACH link
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=$10000 (exact boundary), beneficiary_id=<valid_ach_id>, customer_id=<verified_customer>
expected: Batch queued successfully without requiring step-up verification
trace: R-FIN-004: Boundary case – amount exactly at $10,000 limit (BVA on-point)
ISO 29119: Boundary Value Analysis – test upper boundary of valid range
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_ach_transfer_at_boundary_10000_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 10000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_456',
        'customer_id': 'cust_verified_002',
        'description': 'Boundary test at $10,000'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['amount'] == 10000
    assert data['status'] == 'queued'
    assert 'transfer_id' in data

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds
preconditions: ACH account link exists and is verified
trace: R-FIN-004: Negative case – insufficient funds error pathway
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 5000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_789',
        'customer_id': 'cust_low_balance',
        'description': 'Insufficient funds transfer'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['error_code'] == 'INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS'
    assert 'balance' in data['message'].lower()

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_with_stepup_success
preconditions: ACH account link exists and is verified
Customer has completed step-up verification (MFA challenge passed)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=$15000, beneficiary_id=<valid_ach_id>, customer_id=<verified_customer>, stepup_token=<valid_mfa_token>
trace: R-FIN-004: Positive case – amount exceeds $10,000 WITH step-up verification
ISO 29119: Decision Table – (amount > 10000 AND stepup_verified=true) → 201 accept
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_ach_transfer_exceeds_limit_with_stepup_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 15000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_123',
        'customer_id': 'cust_verified_001',
        'description': 'Over-limit transfer with stepup',
        'stepup_token': '<valid_mfa_token_xyz>'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert data['amount'] == 15000
    assert data['status'] == 'queued'
    assert 'transfer_id' in data

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_unverified_ach_link
preconditions: ACH account link exists but is NOT verified (pending state)
expected: Error message indicates ACH link must be verified first
trace: R-FIN-004: Precondition failure – ACH link not in verified state
ISO 29119: State Transition Coverage – ACH link state validation before transfer
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_ach_transfer_unverified_ach_link():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    payload = {
        'amount': 5000,
        'currency': 'USD',
        'beneficiary_id': 'ach_link_pending',
        'customer_id': 'cust_verified_003',
        'description': 'Transfer with unverified link'
    }
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_token>'}
    resp = requests.post(
        f'{base_url}/transfers/ach',
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'NOT_VERIFIED' in data['error_code']
